taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about the ear, hearing, and equilibrium.
textField:
The external auditory meatus is approximately *2.5* cm long in adults and comprises an outer cartilaginous one-third and an inner *bony* two-thirds. The skin of the bony meatus is thin and tightly adherent to the periosteum, making infections here very painful.

To straighten the EAM for otoscopy in adults, the pinna is pulled *upward* and backward; in children under 3, it is pulled *downward* and backward. This difference is due to the more horizontal orientation of the canal in young children.

The three ossicles of the middle ear are the *malleus*, incus, and *stapes*. The stapes footplate sits in the *oval* window and transmits vibrations to the perilymph of the inner ear. The *tensor tympani* muscle is innervated by CN V3 and dampens sound by tensing the tympanic membrane.

Myringotomy is performed in the *posteroinferior* quadrant of the tympanic membrane to avoid the chorda tympani nerve and ossicles. The *cochlea* is the organ of hearing, while the *semicircular canals* detect rotational acceleration for equilibrium.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the anatomy of the ear and hearing apparatus.
Good effort! Review the details you missed.
